acceptance_criteria: Backend deployment posture is expressed as wasmCloud on Wasmtime with deployable component metadata instead of native service binaries.
The OS shell subset is rehomed under ui/ and exposes only the governed release apps for this product profile.
SurrealDB access is centralized behind shared repository abstractions and UI data access stays on typed SDK/contracts.
Workspace tooling, CI, and process validation encode the new ui and wasmCloud-first rules.

open_questions.question: Nomad host/service topology can be refined further once real lattice rollout automation is introduced.

objective: Sequence the code, tooling, and documentation changes for the integrated architecture.
change_slices: Rehome the shell-first OS subset into ui/ and trim the release registry to Control Center, Terminal, and Settings.
Add WIT, wasmCloud bindings, lattice config, typed SDK transport, and shared SurrealDB repositories.
Replace strategy sandbox trait-object loading with a Wasmtime-backed runtime path and keep in-memory execution feature-gated.
Add xtask, Cargo aliases, CI coverage, Nomad lattice manifests, and process-method ui evidence enforcement.
test_scenarios: Run targeted cargo check passes for the new platform, ui, workflow, and service crates.
Run process-method tests covering the new ui profile evidence requirements.
Build the browser/Tauri shell packages to confirm the rehomed ui workspace composes cleanly.

notes: Wasmtime is the default strategy runtime and wasmCloud component metadata is versioned in platform/.
The new WIT and lattice metadata are additive v1 interfaces.
Parity and smoke tests cover the generated binding and component metadata path.
Service component descriptors point back to canonical service boundaries and schema references.
UI code does not bypass service-owned data or schema boundaries.
Workflow component descriptors keep orchestration failure handling within workflow crates.
Workflow boundaries remain explicit and compensating behavior stays in orchestration code.
The quant strategy promotion flow still routes approvals through existing policy-aware services.
Workflow event and evidence emission remains contract-bound.
SurrealDB repositories, process-method validation, and xtask wiring reuse shared crates instead of copying logic into services or ui.
Shared crates remain generic and do not embed business policy branching.
UI host concerns stay in ui/ and platform_host adapters; service/workflow crates do not own desktop logic.
Browser preview and Tauri desktop builds are both retained in the task runner and CI flow.
Control Center and runtime composition consume typed SDK dashboard snapshots instead of direct database clients.
Nomad manifests deploy lattice support services and hosts, not raw business-service binaries.

rollout_notes: Land the shared runtime/schema/tooling changes before relying on the updated CI paths in new pull requests.
